item.title: ASML raises 2026 outlook on AI chip boom
item.description: Europe’s most valuable company says customers are accelerating expansion plans

item.title: Standard Life buys Aegon UK for £2bn
item.description: Deal comes as large insurers and asset managers compete more aggressively for UK pension assets

item.title: Australian billionaire Gina Rinehart and Rio Tinto told to pay mining royalties to rivals
item.description: Court dismisses some claims but says payments are owed after 16-year legal dispute over iron ore mines

item.title: Middle East war cuts Nissan’s regional car sales by half
item.description: Estimates are clearest indication yet of Iran conflict’s impact on automotive sector

item.title: China told Maersk and MSC to drop Panama port operations
item.description: European shipping groups took over canal concessions after Panamanian authorities ejected Hong Kong’s CK Hutchison

item.title: FirstFT: Iran used Chinese spy satellite to target US bases
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: Bifs replace Piigs, and Lufthansa strikes

item.title: Iran faces halt to oil production within weeks if US blockade succeeds
item.description: Tehran has a maximum of 16 days of storage before it will need to curtail its output

item.title: Strikes at Lufthansa threaten efforts to capitalise on Gulf disruption
item.description: Travel turmoil caused by industrial action a ‘significant handicap’ as carrier tries to benefit from war

item.title: Amazon enters the space race
item.description: The ecommerce giant has agreed an $11.6bn deal to acquire satellite group Globalstar

item.title: Can the traditional British tabloid survive the digital age?
item.description: Social media feeds, AI summaries and influencer-driven content are luring readers away

item.title: The CEO chatbot era is coming
item.description: Many executives will be tempted by Meta’s plans for AI version of Mark Zuckerberg with interest

item.title: Iran used Chinese spy satellite to target US bases
item.description: Leaked documents show IRGC secretly acquired system and used it to guide strikes during war in March

item.title: US hotels slash summer room rates as World Cup demand falls short
item.description: Industry executives say fans are being put off by expensive match tickets, inflation fears and anti-American sentiment

item.title: Oil shortages are coming, and with them some difficult questions
item.description: It may not be the early sufferers who experience the most pain if the Strait of Hormuz stays closed for a prolonged period

item.title: Betting on risk changes the world
item.description: Prediction markets are the latest example of a disruptive new industry smacking into problems incumbents have long understood

item.title: Uber commits $10bn to robotaxis in strategy shift
item.description: Ride-hailing app races to make up lost ground with equity investments and vehicle order commitments

item.title: Democrats cautioned against angering $300mn AI lobby ahead of US midterms
item.description: Pro-industry campaign groups deploy millions amid growing public support for tighter regulation

item.title: China shock 2.0: should Europe repel Chinese investment?
item.description: Brussels wants firms opening factories in the EU to transfer knowledge and employ local staff. Will it work?

item.title: Private healthcare in the UK: helping or harming the NHS?
item.description: Critics worry the country is heading towards a two-tier system. Dentistry is a cautionary tale

item.title: PwC plans overhaul of consulting business
item.description: Shake-up spurred by rise of AI and threat of major upheaval across the industry

item.title: Maine becomes first US state to pass data centre construction ban
item.description: Closely watched legislation could provide a blueprint for others considering moratoriums

item.title: Kirkland & Ellis set to poach top Wachtell distressed debt lawyer
item.description: Firm offers Joshua Feltman a guaranteed pay package of $80mn over three years

item.title: UK’s Help to Buy housing scheme mainly benefited higher earners, says think tank
item.description: Report by IFS says policy did little to increase affordability for those on lower incomes

item.title: Pastries, but not kebabs, fall foul of UK junk food advertising ban
item.description: Watchdog bans Lidl pastry advertisement while items from kebab chain are cleared

item.title: OpenAI releases new cyber security model to limited group of customers
item.description: Launch of GPT-5.4-Cyber follows concern about ability of Anthropic’s Mythos to find software bugs

item.title: The ‘deep fried’ stocks more expensive than Nvidia
item.description: Indonesian regulators investigating ‘broad range of potential market misconduct’

item.title: Tankers transiting Strait of Hormuz stop or turn around amid US blockade, data shows
item.description: US military says no ships pass through its operation to seal off Iranian ports in first 24 hours

item.title: Kevin Warsh is very rich, or possibly very, very rich
item.description: In the Fed with the bread

item.title: Musk is officially free to roam in Texas
item.description: Delaware ruling draws a line under Tesla’s corporate domicile in the Lone Star State

item.title: How the US can blockade the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: US forces will be ready to descend from helicopters on to oil tankers
